title: Salary Into Checking
scenario: A customer receives monthly salary into a checking account and uses it for rent, groceries, and digital payments.
what_to_notice: Checking accounts prioritize access and convenience more than interest earnings.

title: Emergency Fund In Savings
scenario: A customer keeps several months of expenses in a savings account to prepare for uncertainty.
what_to_notice: Savings accounts are useful when safety and modest interest matter more than daily transactions.

title: Car Loan Approval
scenario: A bank reviews income, existing debt, and repayment history before approving a car loan.
what_to_notice: The lender cares about whether the borrower can and will repay.

title: Busy Withdrawal Day
scenario: Many customers withdraw cash on the same day, so the bank must meet demand without disruption.
what_to_notice: Banks need liquid funds because deposits may be withdrawn before loans are repaid.

title: Card Payment At A Store
scenario: A customer taps a debit card at a grocery store and the payment is settled through the banking system.
what_to_notice: A simple customer payment can involve multiple ledgers and institutions behind the scenes.
